mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market recovery, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of triggered months, or the signal produces negative alpha after transaction costs in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rising INDPRO observations, or the signal fails to generate positive rank correlation during confirmed recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >50% of high-inflation trigger months, or the negative predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS remains anchored near zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate wage income and consumption capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >55% of months with positive payroll growth, or the signal information coefficient drops below 0.01 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with rising retail sales confirms robust domestic demand, prompting institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 5-day returns in >60% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals supply-side weakness and impending earnings downgrades, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >50% of contraction trigger months, or the signal fails to predict returns during confirmed contraction regimes.
